summary: Kenneth demonstrates how to roll cash-secured puts using HIMS as a real example, showing the buy-to-close and sell-to-open process to avoid unfavorable assignment. He turned a potential $17,100 loss into a $98 net loss by rolling the position one week further out at a different strike price.
takeaways: Rolling CSP involves two steps: buy-to-close the existing position, then sell-to-open a new series one week further out
Can roll to a higher strike price if bullish on the stock (HIMS example: rolled from $57 to $52 strike)
Rolling premium ($985 in HIMS case) helps offset the buy-to-close loss ($1,897)
Net loss on HIMS roll was $98 ($814 initial premium - $1,897 buy-to-close + $985 new premium), vastly better than taking assignment at $57 when stock was at $51-52
Avoid selling CSP into earnings events due to high volatility risk
Use roll functionality in brokerage platform: click 'roll selected' to access buy-to-close and sell-to-open in one workflow

strategy_candidates.change: Establish a rolling threshold rule: when a CSP goes ITM and assignment is unfavorable, automatically roll out one week if the net debit is less than 20% of the strike price (roughly $11.40 in a $57 strike situation)
strategy_candidates.evidence: HIMS example shows rolling cost $98 net (1.7% of $5,700 risk capital), demonstrating rolling can be capital-efficient vs. taking assignment; Kenneth avoided $17,100 loss by rolling for $98

title: Part 10.5 -  8/8/25 how to roll a cash secured put
